source.multipart_basis: Sample prompt explicitly says 'single CAD assembly (socket shell + pyramid-receiver plate + pyramid adapter + pylon)' as four distinct bodies bonded/bolted together; pressure and ISO 10328 loads traverse these interfaces.

full_prompt: Design one representative total-surface-bearing (TSB) transtibial prosthetic socket + integrated pylon adapter per the Open Prosthetics Project (OPP) guidance and qualified to ISO 10328 P5 loads at the pylon interface, delivered AS A MULTI-PART ASSEMBLY of four distinct bodies: (1) carbon-epoxy socket shell, (2) pyramid-receiver laminate plate, (3) 30 mm male pyramid adapter, (4) aluminum pylon. The socket is a carbon-fiber-reinforced epoxy laminate (4 layers nylon stockinette + 2 layers 6-oz carbon + 4 layers nylon, total wall thickness ~3.5 mm in the wall and ~5 mm at the distal cup) shaped to a representative residual-limb positive (tibial length 150 mm, circumference at MPT 340 mm, circumference at trimline 420 mm). The distal end has a 4-hole pyramid-receiver laminate plate bonded/laminated in, accepting a standard 30 mm male pyramid adapter coupling to a 30 mm aluminum pylon 250 mm long. Two independent verification load cases apply: (A) SOCKET PRESSURE COMFORT CHECK - under static 100 kg body-weight loading in mid-stance, the peak interfacial contact pressure between the socket wall and the residual limb must not exceed 300 kPa over any sensitive region (patellar tendon bar, distal tibia, fibular head) per OPP / ISPO comfort guidance; 150 kPa is the target peak in pressure-tolerant regions (shaft of tibia, medial gastrocnemius). This is evaluated with a soft-tissue + socket contact FEA using a hyperelastic limb model. (B) PYLON STRUCTURAL CHECK - the pyramid receiver + distal cup + pylon assembly must pass the ISO 10328 P5 ultimate static toe and heel tests (3360 N toe / 4000 N heel along the Annex A test lines) without fracture or permanent deformation exceeding clause limits. Target materials: carbon-epoxy laminate (tensile strength ~500 MPa in fiber direction, conservative design strength 250 MPa per OPP), 6061-T6 aluminum pylon + pyramid (yield 276 MPa). Joinery: pyramid-receiver plate is laminated/co-bonded into the socket distal cup; pyramid adapter engages the receiver via four M6 socket-head cap screws tightened to 12 N*m; pylon is clamped to pyramid base via standard pyramid clamp adapter with a single M8 pinch bolt. Deliverable is a single CAD multi-part assembly suitable for (a) nonlinear contact FEA with hyperelastic limb for socket-pressure check, (b) linear-elastic structural FEA of pyramid-receiver + pylon for ISO 10328 P5, with the assembled-only FEM gate reporting across all four part families. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation analyses NOT required.
short_prompt:
Design a transtibial prosthetic socket + pylon + foot assembly per the OPP programme and ISO 10328 P5 proof testing.

Socket carbon-epoxy ultimate 250 MPa; pylon Al 6061-T6 yield 276 MPa; design body mass 100 kg.

Load cases: LC1 ISO 10328 P5 toe load 3360 N; LC2 ISO 10328 P5 heel load 4000 N; LC3 standing vertical 981 N; LC4 socket residual-limb interface peak 300 kPa.

Pass/fail: no material yield / rupture at P5 proof loads; FEM at assembled level; socket-pylon and pylon-foot joint DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Socket interfacial pressure requires a hyperelastic (Ogden / Mooney-Rivlin) residual-limb model in contact with the rigid socket; not standard linear-elastic static.

prompt.title: OPP TSB transtibial socket + pylon to ISO 10328 P5 (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a carbon-epoxy TSB socket + pyramid-receiver plate + pyramid adapter + 30 mm pylon as a four-part assembly passing OPP socket pressure limits (<= 300 kPa peak) and ISO 10328 P5 structural tests (3360 N toe / 4000 N heel).

parts.role: carbon-epoxy laminate TSB socket shell contacting the residual limb
parts.notes: Single laminated shell with integral distal cup; receiver plate co-bonded at distal cup.

parts.role: 4-hole laminated receiver plate accepting the male pyramid adapter

geometric_constraints.bolt_pattern: 4 x M6 on standard pyramid spacing

parts.notes: Aluminum plate co-laminated/bonded into distal cup and bolted to pyramid adapter.

parts.role: standard 30 mm male pyramid adapter transferring load between receiver and pylon clamp
parts.notes: Engages receiver via 4 x M6 cap screws at 12 N*m and clamps pylon via integrated pyramid clamp.

parts.role: 30 mm aluminum pylon conducting body weight to distal foot adapter
parts.notes: Clamped into pyramid base via single M8 pinch bolt.

load_cases.name: static_body_weight_100kg
load_cases.application: mid-stance vertical load via pylon into distal cup

load_cases.name: ISO10328_P5_toe
load_cases.application: Annex A toe test line at pylon

load_cases.name: ISO10328_P5_heel
load_cases.application: Annex A heel test line at pylon

boundary_conditions.residual_limb: proximal end fixed to mock-up femur plate
boundary_conditions.pylon: clamped at foot adapter end for ISO tests

objective.primary: pass all criteria on the assembled model

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: assembled_only FEM gate runs LC1/LC2/LC3 over the full socket+receiver+pyramid+pylon stack; R1-R5 must all pass on the assembled geometry

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid TET10 socket + hyperelastic limb + rigid pyramid + solid pylon

interfaces.detail: pyramid-receiver plate co-laminated/bonded into distal cup during layup
interfaces.load_path: axial and moment loads from pyramid adapter transferred into socket distal cup

interfaces.detail: 4 x M6 socket-head cap screws at 12 N*m clamping pyramid flange to receiver plate
interfaces.load_path: body-weight and ISO 10328 test loads shared across four-bolt pattern

interfaces.detail: pylon clamped into pyramid base via single M8 pinch bolt with pyramid clamp adapter
interfaces.load_path: ISO 10328 P5 toe/heel line loads carried from pylon into pyramid

fem_check_policy.rationale: ISO 10328 and OPP comfort checks both evaluate end-to-end load paths (limb -> socket -> receiver -> pyramid -> pylon); per-part decompositions cannot certify the device without the continuous assembled contact chain.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Patient-specific limb geometry acquisition (MRI or cast)

uncertainty_flags.note: OPP does not publish a single hard limit; 300 kPa is a conservative upper bound commonly used in ISPO literature, 150 kPa is a comfort target. Confirm per-patient clinician guidance.
Count minima speculated from OPP canonical four-body prosthetic stack; real devices may add suspension liner and foot adapter.

eval_coverage: hyperelastic soft-tissue contact FEA across assembled socket
composite socket laminate design check at socket+receiver interface
ISO 10328 P5 combined with socket comfort on end-to-end assembly
